outputs.Why is the sky blue?: The sky looks blue because of the way sunlight moves through the air. Sunlight is made of many colors, like a rainbow. When sunlight hits the air, it scatters, which means it spreads out in all directions. Blue light scatters more than other colors because it travels in smaller waves. This is why we see a blue sky most of the time. At sunrise and sunset, the sky can look red or orange. That's because the sun is lower and the light has to pass through more air, scattering away the blue light and leaving the reds and oranges for us to see.
outputs.How do plants make their own food?: Plants make their own food using sunlight. They have a special part called leaves. Leaves are like tiny kitchens for the plants. Inside the leaves, there is something called chlorophyll, which makes the leaves green. Chlorophyll helps the plant catch sunlight. The plant then mixes the sunlight with air and water. This mix turns into food for the plant. This whole process is called photosynthesis, which means putting together with light.
outputs.Why do we have day and night?: The Earth is like a big ball in space. This ball spins around and around. It takes one whole day for the Earth to do one full spin. When your part of the Earth faces the sun, it is day. The sun makes everything bright and warm. When your part of the Earth turns away from the sun, it is night. At night, it is dark because the sun's light cannot reach you. So, day and night happen because the Earth keeps spinning.
outputs.What makes ice melt?: Ice is made of water that is frozen. It melts when it gets warm. Warm things have energy that makes tiny parts, called molecules, move faster. When ice gets warm, its molecules move faster too. They start to break apart from each other. This is why ice turns back into water. So, when the sun shines on ice, or you hold it in your hand, the warmth makes it melt.
outputs.How do magnets work?: Magnets are special because they can pull some things toward them. They have two ends called poles. One end is called the north pole and the other is the south pole. If you put the north pole near the south pole of another magnet, they stick together. If you try to put two north poles together, they push away from each other. This happens because of something called magnetic force. Magnetic force is an invisible push or pull that comes from the magnet. Some metals, like iron, can be pulled by magnets because they have tiny pieces inside that line up with the magnet's force.
outputs.Why do things fall to the ground?:
Things fall to the ground because of something called gravity. Gravity is a force, which is like an invisible hand that pulls things down. Imagine you have a magnet that pulls metal towards it. Gravity is like a giant magnet, but it pulls everything, not just metal. 

The Earth is very big and strong, so it has a lot of gravity. This is why when you drop a ball, it falls to the ground. Gravity keeps us on the ground too, so we don't float away. Even the moon stays near Earth because of gravity.
outputs.Where does a puddle go when it dries up?: When a puddle dries up, the water goes into the air. This is called evaporation. Water is made of tiny pieces called molecules. When the sun shines, it makes these molecules move faster. They move so fast that they jump up into the air. We cannot see them when they are in the air. The water changes from a liquid puddle to a gas in the air. This is why the puddle disappears.
outputs.Why do we have seasons?: The Earth goes around the Sun. This trip takes one whole year. The Earth is a little tilted, like when you tilt your head to the side. Because of this tilt, different parts of the Earth get more sunlight at different times of the year. When your part of the Earth gets more sunlight, it is summer. When it gets less sunlight, it is winter. Spring and fall happen in between when the sunlight changes. This is why we have seasons!
outputs.How do our lungs help us breathe?: Our lungs help us breathe in air. When you take a breath in, your lungs fill up with air like a balloon. This air has something called oxygen, which your body needs. Your lungs take the oxygen from the air and send it to your blood. Your blood carries the oxygen all around your body to help you move and play. When you breathe out, your lungs push out air that your body doesn't need anymore. This air has something called carbon dioxide. We need to get rid of carbon dioxide, so we breathe it out.
outputs.What makes a rainbow?: A rainbow happens when sunlight hits raindrops in the sky. The light from the sun looks white, but it is made of many colors. When the light goes through the raindrop, it bends and splits into these colors. The colors are red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple. This bending and splitting make a rainbow. You can only see a rainbow when the sun is behind you and it is raining in front of you. Rainbows are like nature's way of showing all the colors in sunlight.
outputs.Why does the moon look like it changes s: The moon looks like it changes shape because of how it moves around Earth. The moon does not make its own light. It shines because the sun lights it up. As the moon goes around Earth, we see different parts of the lit-up side. Sometimes we see a big round moon. This is called a full moon. Other times, we see just a small part, like a slice. This is called a crescent moon. The shapes change all the time because the moon is always moving.
outputs.How do fish breathe underwater?: Fish breathe underwater using their gills. Gills are special body parts that take oxygen from the water. Fish open their mouths to let water in. The water then flows over the gills. Gills have tiny parts that grab oxygen from the water. The oxygen moves into the fish's blood. This is how fish get the air they need to live. Fish don't need to come up for air like we do.
